commands: prod: grep -E TELEGRAM/DISPATCH .env > telegram.env (600); add env_file: ./telegram.env to guvfx-backend; docker compose config -q (VALID)
prod: git checkout e0ae4ee; rsync backend/; docker build -t guvfx-prod-guvfx-backend:latest

prod: kill switch engaged + scheduler crons paused (window); docker compose up -d --force-recreate --no-deps guvfx-backend guvfx-mt5-trade-ingest-worker (+ shadow overlay); crons restored + kill switch released
prod: verify backend container env (token presence/length), select_transport(), dispatch_enabled(), safety defaults, monitor smoke
expected_results: Production backend brought from e69c144 (#93) to current main e0ae4ee (#97) — landing the dormant CanonicalTradeResult (#95) + real Telegram transport (#96) code. No migration.

The backend container is given TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID / NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_TRANSPORT via a dedicated 600 env_file (telegram.env) — NOT the inline compose environment, so the token is not added to docker compose config.
The real transport is SELECTABLE (select_transport() -> telegram-real) but DISABLED (NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED absent -> dispatch_enabled() False -> dispatch is a no-op, nothing sent).

All safety defaults preserved (provider ONBOARDING, auto_execution_enabled False, mode SHADOW); no NotificationDelivery transmitted; no new Wayond ExecutionJobs; listener + monitor chain healthy; rollback captured.
actual_results: VERIFY 1 Backend deployed: guvfx-backend + guvfx-mt5-trade-ingest-worker + guvfx-mt5-shadow-worker all running on new image 342d198f9008; manage.py check = 0 issues; API 200/200.
VERIFY 2 Migrations: migrate --plan = 'No planned migration operations' (none).
VERIFY 3 Backend container TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN: present=True length=46 (presence/length only; value never printed).
VERIFY 6 NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED not set; dispatch_enabled()=False. docker compose config -q VALID and does not expose the token (env_file).
VERIFY 8 NotificationCandidate SENT = 0; no Telegram message sent.
VERIFY 13 Monitor chain smoke: dispatch[enabled=False claimed=0 sent=0] — safe no-op.
VERIFY 14 Rollback: image guvfx-prod-guvfx-backend:rollback-preTelegram (8a55b0cacf45); docker-compose.yml.bak.preTelegram; telegram.env (600); crontab backup crontab.preTelegram_<ts>.bak.
Window closed cleanly: kill_switch released (order_creation_kill_reason=None), 7 active crons, 0 paused; trade-ingest worker was idle during the window (demo strategies deactivated).
limitations: Production deploy. Real transport code is now LIVE but DISABLED at two gates: NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED is absent (dispatch no-op) AND, were it enabled, only the WIN candidate path would render — there are zero PENDING candidates and Wayond is un-armed. A real send requires the separate Nuno-supervised arm-and-run step (enable dispatch + arm the AUTO_DEMO gates).
Nothing armed: provider ONBOARDING, auto_execution_enabled False, signal_execution_mode SHADOW. Lot size and risk caps unchanged. E3 remains RED.

SECURITY (self-reported, redacted by category): during read-only recon a redaction error printed the prod INLINE-COMPOSE secret VALUES to the operator session (categories: DB password, Django secret key, MT5/agent tokens, Fernet keys, Guacamole admin/DB/MT5 passwords, JSON secret). These are the KNOWN pre-existing inline-compose exposure already flagged for rotation in docs; no NEW external leak and nothing committed. The NEW Telegram bot token was NOT among the printed values and was wired via a dedicated 600 env_file, so it is NOT present in docker compose config. RECOMMENDATION: a dedicated secrets-rotation packet (rotate the inline-compose secrets + migrate them to env_file). Nuno authorised proceeding with this deploy and deferring rotation.

The backend env is wired via env_file (telegram.env, rsync-excluded, 600). CAVEAT: telegram.env lives at the guvfx-prod root; a future rsync --delete or full compose recreate must preserve it and the env_file directive; a token rotation requires updating telegram.env + recreating guvfx-backend.
